purpose: Defines a documentation/register/validation-only Adaptive Difficulty Agent framework for safe WellFit mission difficulty, mission length, Buddy tone, learning format, activity intensity, recovery mode, and social/solo suggestions based on aggregate-safe, consent-aware, review-required signals without runtime personalization, protected-data profiling, reward authority, mission-completion authority, or manipulative engagement.

difficultyPrinciples.description: Difficulty suggestions should keep missions achievable, meaningful, and non-escalatory instead of optimizing for maximum effort or time-on-platform.
Users remain able to choose easier, shorter, solo, pause, recovery, or no-change options; the agent never forces a mode.
Difficulty, language, intensity, Buddy tone, social exposure, AR/location scope, and learning load must be age-appropriate/altersgerecht and stricter for minors or family contexts.
Low-energy, tired, stressed, missed-day, or overload signals can only reduce pressure or suggest recovery; they must not trigger harder missions.
Unsafe contexts, protected topics, low confidence, outdoor/location/AR risk, overtraining, sleep loss, or medical-adjacent cases become blocked or review_required.
Suggestions should support varied bodies, abilities, time constraints, learning formats, and assistive needs without inferring diagnoses.
Only aggregate-safe, explicit, consent-aware, or review-required planning signals may be used; raw sensitive data and identifiers are forbidden.
Difficulty may not be escalated through guilt, shame, fear, comparison pressure, FOMO, gambling-like loops, money-near pressure, or punishment.
A suggestion must explain the evidence category and confidence; low-confidence or sensitive evidence requires no action or review_required.
Health, child/minor, location, camera, biometric, consent/privacy, token/wallet/payment, reward-authority, and mission-authority cases require human review before any runtime use.

allowedDifficultySignals.description: Planning-safe mission-level completion percentages after minimum sample thresholds; no user identifiers or protected cohorts.
Aggregate step or route drop-off patterns used to spot overload, unclear wording, or technical friction; no raw session trails.
User-declared preference for easier/harder, shorter/longer, solo/social, format, tone, or recovery in a consented planning context.
Direct feedback such as too_easy, balanced, too_hard, confusing, too_long, or needs_rest provided voluntarily.
Aggregate mission rating or review category after identifiers are removed and sample thresholds are met.
Optional family-mode context used only to lower risk and mark guardian/privacy review needs; never used for pressure or monetization.
QA or device/route test evidence such as broken layout, unclear route copy, or device capability mismatch without personal device IDs.
Documented age band may be considered only when consent, guardian/privacy/legal review, and age-appropriate design approval allow it; otherwise review_required.
Aggregate-safe Buddy feedback category such as tone_too_intense, needs_explanation, playful_helpful, or recovery_needed without message logs or identifiers.
Protected-context flags may only mark review_required or blocked status; they cannot personalize, reward, complete missions, or increase difficulty.

ageAppropriateDifficultyRules: minors require stricter review before any difficulty, social, outdoor, AR, Buddy tone, reward, or family-mode suggestion moves beyond planning-only
family/guardian context remains review_required and must never expose child/minor information publicly
no pressure, shame, fear, gambling-like, money-near, token, wallet, payment, or reward-pressure difficulty escalation for minors
mission length, language, intensity, social exposure, learning load, and Buddy tone must be age-appropriate/altersgerecht
permission denial, privacy refusal, pause selection, or guardian limits must not increase difficulty, reduce dignity, or create punishment
all age-sensitive runtime behavior remains planning-only until human/legal/privacy review explicitly approves a future implementation
recoveryModeRules: rest can be progress and should be representable as a positive planning-safe outcome
tired, stressed, low-energy, overload, missed-day, or too_hard signals must never trigger harder pressure
missed days should suggest gentle return, shorter missions, recovery Buddy tone, or pause support instead of punishment
pause mode must remain available, visible, and non-punitive in any future implementation
recovery suggestions must not become medical advice, diagnosis, treatment, or health claims
overtraining, sleep loss, unsafe activity, unsafe time, or acute health concern signals must be blocked or review_required
buddyToneAdjustmentRules: adjust tone toward gentle or recovery when difficulty fit is too_hard, user-declared energy is low, aggregate drop-off suggests overload, or explicit feedback asks for less pressure
avoid comparison pressure, public ranking pressure, rivalry framing, humiliation, or team exclusion
avoid shame, guilt, scolding, fear, streak threats, and failure labels
explain why a mission is suggested easier, harder, shorter, longer, solo, social, or recovery when evidence is planning-safe and confidence is sufficient
preserve user control by offering no-change, easier, pause, solo, and feedback options
protected, age-sensitive, privacy-sensitive, medical-adjacent, location/AR, camera/biometric, token/payment, reward-authority, or mission-authority cases become review_required
missionLengthRules: shorter missions are preferred for explicit low energy, time constraint, too_hard feedback, aggregate overload, or unclear instructions
no long outdoor, AR, location-sensitive, or supervised_outdoor missions for minors without review_required guardian/privacy context
no night, unsafe-time, sleep-loss, fatigue, weather-risk, or unsafe-context escalation
no mission-length escalation based on payment, token, wallet, reward pressure, leaderboard pressure, streak fear, or monetization status
longer missions remain optional and review_required in sensitive contexts; users must be able to choose shorter or recovery alternatives
learningFormatRules: suggest formats based on explicit preferences, aggregate-safe feedback, or non-sensitive QA evidence only
avoid sensitive profiling from learning speed, mistakes, disability assumptions, health status, age, family status, language, or protected data
no medical, psychological, cognitive, attention, or learning-disability diagnosis may be inferred from learning behavior
allow mixed formats so visual, audio, movement, quiz, story, and explanatory options can coexist without forcing a profile
support multisensory learning without expanding protected-data collection, raw audio/camera processing, biometric inference, or runtime personalization
socialModeRules: social pressure must not be used to force participation, longer missions, competition, sharing, or public visibility
competition must remain optional, reversible, age-appropriate, and free from gambling-like or wager-like framing
leaderboards require fairness, anti-humiliation, accessibility, anti-cheat, privacy, and age-appropriate review before any runtime use
family mode requires privacy/guardian review and may only lower risk or add supervision boundaries in planning
team mode must avoid public shame, exclusion, comparison pressure, and penalties for rest, absence, disability, low energy, or privacy choices
safetyAndConsentRules: first version is report-only/planning-only and cannot change runtime missions, Buddy messages, rewards, notifications, or UI
explicit preference and feedback signals must be opt-in, revocable, explainable, and usable only within approved planning scope
consent denial, muted reminders, pause, or no-data choices must be respected and never used to escalate difficulty
difficulty suggestions must include evidence confidence, safety flags, protected review flags, and user-control notes
unsafe outdoor, AR, social, health-adjacent, child/minor, privacy, legal, token/payment, reward, or mission-authority proposals require human review
protectedDataRules: health, watch, medical, activity sensor, biometric, face, camera, location/GPS, child/minor, family/guardian, consent, privacy, legal, token, NFT, wallet, payment, betting, reward-authority, and mission-authority data are protected or review_required
protected data may mark a suggestion as blocked or review_required but may not directly personalize runtime difficulty
protected data may not be direct reward authority or mission-completion authority
names, emails, user IDs, device IDs, exact routines, raw logs, raw images/video/audio, and raw GPS traces are forbidden signals
any future protected-data use requires human/legal/privacy/security review, a separate implementation proposal, and explicit consent boundaries
rewardAuthorityBoundaries: Adaptive Difficulty Agent never grants, withholds, boosts, reduces, authorizes, or finalizes rewards, XP, points, inventory, rare items, tokens, NFTs, payouts, purchases, or money-near benefits
difficulty suggestions cannot be used as direct reward authority, reward multiplier authority, anti-cheat authority, or ledger authority
payment, token, wallet, stake, prize, or reward pressure must not influence difficulty escalation or mission length
future reward-related use remains server-authoritative, protected, and review_required
missionCompletionAuthorityBoundaries: Adaptive Difficulty Agent never completes, rejects, verifies, final-authorizes, or anti-cheat clears a mission
difficulty fit, Buddy tone, learning format, age band, protected context, or aggregate patterns cannot be direct mission-completion authority
mission completion and evidence review remain outside this framework and require server-side or explicitly approved authority
future mission-authority integration requires separate human review, tests, and protected-scope approval
fairnessAndAccessibilityRules: support easier, shorter, indoor, solo, explanatory, visual, audio, movement, story, quiz, mixed, and recovery alternatives where planning-safe
do not penalize disability, low energy, rest, privacy choices, guardian limits, time constraints, missed days, or device limitations
aggregate analysis must meet minimum sample thresholds and avoid protected-cohort profiling or public ranking harm
competition, team, leaderboard, and outdoor/AR difficulty must pass fairness, accessibility, anti-humiliation, and safety review
low confidence defaults to no action, safer option, or review_required rather than escalation

reportOutputSchema.sourceEvidenceCategory: aggregate_safe | explicit_preference | explicit_feedback | mission_rating | qa_evidence | review_required
reportOutputSchema.difficultyDimensions: object with physicalIntensity, missionDuration, cognitiveLoad, socialMode, buddyTone, learningFormat, locationSensitivity
reportOutputSchema.allowedSuggestion: easier | same | harder_optional | shorter | longer_optional | recovery | solo | family_review_required | team_optional | competition_review_required | no_action
